evals.prompt: Plan and implement a multi-step feature in this repo, and run verification as its own lane.
evals.expected_output: A manager-led plan that runs the orchestrator loop (intake → triage → plan → implement → verify) and splits verification into a separate review lane rather than doing everything in one undifferentiated pass.
assertions: Establishes the orchestrator loop with explicit steps
Opens a distinct verification/review lane separate from implementation
Keeps a single visible owner integrating the lanes

evals.prompt: Fix this one-line typo in the README.
evals.expected_output: Recognized as trivial and handled directly on the main path — no triage/plan/verification lanes and no routed-work ceremony for a one-line change. (The lightweight run trail the runtime always writes is expected; the point is no lane orchestration.)
assertions: Recognizes the task as trivial and stays on the direct path (does not run the full routed loop)
Does not spawn subagent lanes for a one-step change

evals.prompt: Two parts of this change touch different modules and can proceed at once. Run them in parallel safely.
evals.expected_output: A response that opens disjoint implementation lanes with explicit non-overlapping write scopes so concurrent edits cannot conflict, and states why the parallel split is worth the overhead.
assertions: Assigns each lane a disjoint, explicit write set so parallel edits cannot conflict
States why the parallel split is worth the overhead

evals.prompt: Have a reviewer check the diff for correctness and run the tests, but it must not be able to change any code.
evals.expected_output: A verification lane mapped to the read-only code-reviewer agent that can run build/test and review the diff but has no file-mutating tools.
assertions: Routes to the code-reviewer lane
Confirms the lane is read-only (no Edit/Write) and cannot modify the repo
Returns a structured verdict / findings rather than edits

evals.prompt: After the change is implemented, open a pull request and post a comment on the linked ticket.
evals.expected_output: A response that prepares the PR narrative and ticket draft locally but gates the actual PR creation and ticket comment on explicit user approval, keeping the developer as the credited actor.
assertions: Treats PR creation and ticket comment as outward-facing, approval-gated actions
Drafts the content but does not publish without approval
Preserves the developer as the credited author

evals.prompt: I want to change the auth flow in a repo you haven't seen. Reduce the chance of guessing wrong before editing.
evals.expected_output: A discovery / repo-understanding lane (Explore) that maps the runtime, entrypoints, and edit surfaces first, treating discovery as first-class bounded work before implementation begins.
assertions: Runs a discovery/repo-understanding pass before editing
Maps runtime family, modules, and likely edit surfaces
Defers code changes until the unknowns are reduced

evals.prompt: Coordinate the research lane and the implementation lane so they share findings as they work.
evals.expected_output: A response that keeps the coordinator (hub-and-spoke) model: lanes report back to the manager, which passes needed context into the next lane's prompt, rather than implying lanes message each other directly.
assertions: Mediates cross-lane information through the manager, not peer-to-peer
Does not promise direct lane-to-lane messaging
Keeps the manager as the single integration point

evals.prompt: Delegate the implementation lane, but this repo has no custom implementer agent defined.
evals.expected_output: A response that falls back to the built-in general-purpose subagent for the implementation lane while preserving the same handoff shape and write-scope discipline.
assertions: Falls back to general-purpose when the custom lane agent is absent
Still applies the lane handoff shape (owned files, objective, verification ask)

evals.prompt: The implementation is done but the test suite is failing. Decide whether the work is complete.
evals.expected_output: A response that treats failing tests as a verification failure, enters the recover step with a revised strategy, and does not declare the work done.
assertions: Does not declare the task complete while tests fail
Routes to the recover step / backtracks with a revised approach
Reports the failure with evidence rather than hiding it

evals.prompt: This is substantial multi-step routed work. Explain what trail you keep so it is auditable.
evals.expected_output: A response that preserves a proportional run ledger (runId, taskType, target paths, plan, per-lane results, approval decisions, acceptance evidence) and the explicit checkpoints, while keeping the trail light for trivial work.
assertions: Describes a run ledger with the core fields and per-lane results
Names explicit checkpoints (intake-ready → plan-ready → implementation-ready → verified → closed)
Scales the artifact trail to the size of the work

evals.prompt: I need a focused answer on how a version-sensitive library API should be used in this repo before we rely on it.
evals.expected_output: A research lane mapped to the researcher agent that grounds the answer in the repo's actual versions first, cites evidence, and returns a confidence-rated, read-only conclusion.
assertions: Routes to the researcher lane (read-only)
Grounds the answer in the repo's actual versions/manifests before external sources
Returns a cited answer with a confidence rating

evals.prompt: An automated, repeatable rule: always run the test suite before any change is declared done.
evals.expected_output: A response that places the repeatable gate in a Claude Code hook (settings.json) rather than relying on prose the model must remember, while still treating failed tests as a verification failure in the loop.
assertions: Recommends a hook for the automated, repeatable gate
Does not rely solely on skill prose to enforce an always-run rule
